items.title: Metamaterials enable control of heat transfer at nanoscale, potentially transforming energy and electronics
items.summary: Heat behaves in predictable ways: a hot cup of coffee cools, a laptop warms your hands, the sun heats Earth. But at scales thousands of times smaller than a human hair, those rules begin to break down, and scientists are learning how to take advantage of that.

items.title: Where are all the intermediate mass black holes? Microlensing fast radio bursts might reveal them
items.summary: Astrophysicists think that black hole masses are hierarchical. The largest are supermassive black holes (SMBH) like the one at the center of the Milky Way and other galaxies. Stellar mass black holes are born of collapsing stars, and are smaller. The smallest of all are the theoretical primordial bl

items.title: Are the chemicals around you safe? Researchers are using AI to find out
items.summary: People are exposed to thousands of chemicals every day—through the products they use, the food they eat and the environments they live in—but only a fraction of those chemicals have been fully tested for safety.

items.title: Early childhood educators change how they speak to toddlers depending on gender, finds study
items.summary: Early childhood educators speak differently to boys compared to girls in toddler classrooms, University of Queensland research has found.

items.title: Analysis of more than 10,000 cities reveals hidden details governments can use to better support their people
items.summary: The world's urban population increased by 785 million people between 2000 and 2020, but that tells only part of the story. Now, a research team including an expert from the University of Michigan has dug into the demographics of more than 10,000 individual cities to obtain insights that can be

items.title: Corn Belt groundwater and irrigation boost thunderstorm complexes by 24–35%, simulations show
items.summary: An international team of scientists has demonstrated how powerful thunderstorm complexes over the U.S. Corn Belt are fueled by moisture rising from the region's fertile fields or just beneath them. The findings can lead to better and longer-term weather forecasts for this critical farming area

items.title: AI ads are almost indistinguishable from human-made work—they just don't perform as well
items.summary: Ads generated by artificial intelligence are nearly indistinguishable from human-made ones, but new research shows they consistently underperform compared to human-made work when it comes to predicting short-term sales impact.

items.title: Released: NASA Goddard Issues Draft Request for Proposal for the Landsat 10 Spacecraft
items.summary: The Landsat 10 Spacecraft Draft Request for Proposal (DRFP) is available for review via SAM.gov.

items.title: Revised mushroom toxin pathway could improve poisoning detection
items.summary: The St. George's mushroom (Calocybe gambosa) is a popular edible mushroom, usually picked in May. Unfortunately, however, it also carries a risk as it can easily be confused with the young deadly fiber cap, which can potentially lead to fatal poisoning. The reason for this is the mushroom toxin

items.title: DNA assembly insights could streamline design and manufacturing of nanostructures for medicine, materials and more
items.summary: Researchers at The University of Texas at Austin have uncovered key principles that govern how DNA "origami" structures fold, findings that could make nanoscale materials faster and easier to manufacture. DNA origami is a technique that uses strands of DNA to self-assemble into tiny, programmable sh

items.title: Sea squirt reveals glowing spines and unexpected nervous system anatomy
items.summary: Ascidians, also known as sea squirts, are the evolutionary link between vertebrates and invertebrates, making them valuable subjects of biological studies. For the first time, researchers at Ruhr University Bochum have detected pronounced autofluorescence in Halocynthia papillosa, one of more than 3

items.title: Study clarifies conditions for amphibian species richness on marine islands
items.summary: A Brazilian study published in the journal Ecography indicates that the biodiversity of anuran amphibians (toads and frogs) on islands is determined by factors encompassed in two previously opposing theories.

items.title: Long-distance bat migration runs on fatty acids, challenging limits of mammal metabolism
items.summary: Bats are the only mammals that can actively fly, enabling many species to perform seasonal migrations. In migratory birds, remaining airborne for many hours is supported by burning fatty acids, something most mammals are incapable of.

items.title: How developing immune cells fine-tune their signals
items.summary: Researchers at VIB, Ghent University, and VUB have uncovered how two proteins essential for immune cell development work together at the molecular level. The findings provide important insights into a critical mechanism that mediates the integration of molecular signals received from immunological t

items.title: Social background also shapes environmental awareness, says study
items.summary: Academic success depends largely on social background—this has long been known. A recent international study led by researchers at Leuphana University of Lüneburg now shows that young people's attitudes toward environmental protection are also strongly influenced by their social background. As

items.title: New biosynthesis platform could enable bioactive compounds to be labeled, targeted and released on demand
items.summary: Many medically important drugs originate from natural sources. Microorganisms produce these compounds using highly sophisticated and remarkably precise enzymatic assembly lines. Many natural microbial products belong to a class known as nonribosomal peptides—short chains of amino acids that often po

items.title: Q&A: How researchers are building next-gen quantum computers
items.summary: Quantum computers have the potential to transform science, accelerating breakthroughs in drug development, cosmology, materials science, nuclear physics, and more.

items.title: La NASA ofrece información actualizada sobre rovers, módulos de alunizaje y misiones de Base Lunar
items.summary: Durante una sesión informativa sobre el programa Base Lunar, celebrada en la sede de la NASA en Washington, la agencia anunció nuevos contratos para el desarrollo de vehículos lunares con capacidad para transportar tripulación y módulos de aterrizaje de carga no tripulados con destino a la Luna. Dir

items.title: Gutweed successfully grown in full life cycle within brackish conditions in Baltic Sea
items.summary: Gutweed could become a future crop in the Baltic Sea. This is shown in a new study from the University of Gothenburg, where researchers have, for the first time, successfully cultivated the seaweed through its entire life cycle in brackish water.

items.title: A giant warm wave is crossing the Pacific, signaling an El Niño that could alter weather worldwide this year
items.summary: Waves of higher, warmer water move eastward across the Pacific Ocean a few months before an El Niño emerges. Several have shown up in 2026 satellite data.
